items.explain: Trailing & starts the command as a background job.

items.prompt: Ctrl+Z on a foreground job usually…
choices: Suspends it (Stopped) — it can be resumed
items.explain: Ctrl+Z sends SIGTSTP — stop, not kill.

items.prompt: fg brings a job to the foreground; bg resumes a stopped job in the background.
items.explain: fg / bg are the resume directions for shell jobs.

items.prompt: A common pattern for a long sim is…
choices: Run with > log 2>&1 & then inspect with tail
Only use kill -9 with no log
items.explain: Background + log + tail (or tail -f) is the usual workflow.
